column2: We Can Quit asks the same question twice in different wording: medical conditions (Q47/Q49), cardiac (Q48/Q50), pregnancy (Q53/Q54), smoking status (Q17/Q19), GP consult (Q28/Q38). Adds 7 unnecessary fields.
column5: Tabuu has ZERO redundant questions. Every field is asked once.
column8: IMPACT: Removing duplicates cuts WCQ form by ~12% and eliminates user confusion

column2: WCQ Q33-Q37 ask 5 separate vaping habit questions. Many radio groups have 6-9 options when 3-4 would suffice.
column5: Tabuu keeps radio options to 2-4 per question max.
column8: IMPACT: Simplifying options reduces cognitive load and speeds completion by ~40%

column2: WCQ has 5 separate consent checkboxes spread throughout the form (Q32/Q55/Q56/Q57/Q58).
column5: Tabuu has 1 consent checkbox + 1 terms checkbox = 2 total.
column8: IMPACT: One consent block at the end is industry best practice. Tabuu does this right

column2: WCQ Q39-Q46 ask 8 questions about specific vaping devices/strengths/mL/products. Most patients select 'don't know'.
column5: Tabuu asks only 1 vaping product question (Disposable/Refillable/Other).
column8: IMPACT: Removing 7 premature questions and deferring to consultation improves completion

column2: WCQ has 57 question fields with no progress indicator.
column5: Tabuu has ~27 question fields + transition pages with clear section breaks.
column8: IMPACT: "Tabuu's form is 53% shorter. Industry benchmark is 15-25 fields for medical intake"

column2: WCQ does not collect: Government ID upload, Medicare card photo, years smoked, quit motivation reasons.
column5: Tabuu has stronger identity verification (ID upload + Medicare photo) and better TGA compliance (first-line treatment explanation).
column8: IMPACT: Adding ID verification and first-line treatment explanation would strengthen compliance

column2: WCQ collects full residential address (7 fields including Street/City/State/ZIP) at intake.
column5: Tabuu does NOT collect address at intake. Address collected at shipping/prescription stage.
column8: IMPACT: Deferring address to checkout reduces intake form length and friction

column3: ID photo + Medicare photo + Medicare number
